method_atom: a large-N matrix field theory whose partition function is organized as a topological (genus) expansion, in which the leading N-to-infinity (planar) limit captures the dominant non-crossing configurations and successive 1/N^2 corrections systematically add contributions of higher topological genus; a recursion relation on the leading term is generalized to generate the subleading, higher-order configurations.
object_atom: the folding of an RNA biomolecule — its secondary structure (base-pairings), together with tertiary structure and pseudo-knots.
brief_reason: the genus-organized large-N matrix expansion with a planar limit and 1/N^2 corrections plus a recursion relation is the transferable machinery, while the RNA molecule and its secondary/tertiary/pseudo-knot folding are the biology-specific object.
